mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which forces equity markets to price in lower corporate revenues and yields negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when unemployment rises above the threshold over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >50% of cases over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 when annual inflation exceeds 5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 250 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in >65% of months when CPI exceeds the threshold over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting wage growth and consumer demand that pushes S&P 500 returns higher.
failure_condition: Combined signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of observations during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly gross domestic product growth reflects underlying economic expansion that translates into higher corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window or returns are negative in >60% of positive GDP quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing demand, prompting equity sell-offs and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >50% of contraction months over a 5-year sample period.
